title: linanthus bigelovii subsp. bigelovii
intro: Linanthus bigelovii subsp. bigelovii, also known as Bigelow's linanthus or desert pink, is a flowering plant in the phlox family. Native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, this plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Linanthus bigelovii subsp. bigelovii include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce fever, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
